oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Youth Contest Winners
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Corwin, Trinity
Silmon, Timothy
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Why I Want to Breed Eclectus ParrotsHave you ever seen an Eclectus parrot? If you haven’t, go look them up right now — they are seriously amazing!The males are bright green with orange beaks, and the females are red and purple. It’s like someonepainted them with a bunch of neon crayons. When I found out that boy and girl Eclectus parrots look sodifferent, I thought that was the coolest thing ever. That’s when I started thinking about breeding them. I’ve always loved animals, but there’s something really special about birds. Now I’ve decided that I really want to breed Eclectus parrots when I get older (or sooner if I can convince my parents!).
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 52 No. 2 (2025): AFA Watchbird Fall/Winter 2025; 4-5
